danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies specific to time period (e.g., flesh-eating boar in Stone Age, period-appropriate antagonists)
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat or avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive alien antagonist character sprite appearing in multiple time periods
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat/boss encounter; presence indicates critical danger to mission

danger_signals.visual_form: Period-specific obstacles and puzzles blocking progression
danger_signals.meaning: Danger from environment rather than enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs like Cleopatra, Merlin, Sherlock Holmes, Leonardo da Vinci, King Arthur rendered as distinct character sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction zones; these characters provide aid, weapons, and mission guidance

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectable items scattered in explored areas
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to pick up; restores health measured by heart containers

safety_signals.visual_form: Tink's Axe, Cleopatra's Bronze Dagger, Leonardo's Katana, Psychic Shock Wave visual effects
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to acquire; enhances combat capability and unlocks new interaction possibilities

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Space pod design, Tetrad/Block objects (alien technology)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: C-Island location; Argonian settlement visible in final chapter

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Zoda character sprite appearing as Zoda-X, Zoda-Y, Zoda-Z (clone variants)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Same alien leader appears across multiple time periods; clones distinguished by letter designation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present across all time periods attempting to collect Tetrads; final confrontation at C-Island

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Period-appropriate character designs (Egyptian Cleopatra, Medieval Merlin, Victorian Sherlock Holmes, Renaissance Leonardo, Arthurian King Arthur)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Each time period contains one major ally figure; their location marks safe zones within chapters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Primitive environment; caveman tribe character design; flesh-eating boar enemy; stone-age aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mike must rescue cavemen's children; mission-driven combat and exploration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Egyptian architectural styling; Cleopatra character sprite; pyramid/temple environments; hieroglyphic visual language
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cleopatra provides Bronze Dagger weapon; Mike collects associated Tetrad

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Renaissance architecture; Leonardo da Vinci character sprite; mechanical elements; basement laboratory setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Leonardo provides Katana weapon; mechanical ball obstacles in dungeon (weapon mysteriously changes during progression)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle architecture; King Arthur character sprite; medieval enemy designs; fantasy aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King Arthur provides aid; medieval combat encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Victorian-era architecture; Sherlock Holmes character sprite; museum setting; urban Victorian aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Holmes prevents museum robbery; discovers Zoda-X presence; deduces existence of other Zoda clones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gothic architecture; dark atmospheric coloring; Zoda-Y encountered; supernatural setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zoda-Y boss fight; Tetrad collection continues

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tropical island setting (returning to original StarTropics location); Argonian settlement; space pod elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zoda-Z invades; Mike returns to defend; final boss encounter; Tetrads assembled; Hirocon (Argonian leader) revealed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tetrads assembled together; Argonian leader emerges from Tetrad configuration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hirocon explains prior strategic sealing; Argonians depart to rebuild Argonia

environmental_storytelling.example: Each chapter's environment immediately communicates era through architecture, character design, and aesthetic (Stone Age primitives, Egyptian monuments, Renaissance machines, Medieval castles, Victorian London, Gothic Transylvania)

Historical figures (Cleopatra, Merlin, Sherlock Holmes, Leonardo da Vinci, King Arthur) serve as both environmental markers and exposition sources—their presence confirms correct location and provides context
Tetrads (Tetris-piece-like objects) serve as physical manifestations of plot progression; collecting them across time periods tells story of scattered alien technology and reveals final confrontation
Enemies reflect their time period (flesh-eating boar in Stone Age, period-specific antagonists in other eras), creating visual coherence and teaching player about historical setting
Zoda appearing across multiple time periods (as Zoda-X, Zoda-Y, Zoda-Z) as a visual/narrative constant communicates that threat transcends time and is pursuing same mission objective
Final chapter returns to C-Island (original StarTropics setting), visually reinforcing narrative closure and connecting sequel to prequel

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can engage enemies in combat; can interact with environment; some enemies 'react differently depending on which weapon is used'
Can move in eight directions and change direction mid-jump (gameplay enhancement over original)
Actively collecting Tetrads; pursue same objective as Mike; require boss-level combat engagement
Provide weapon upgrades, mission information, and aid; static friendly presence; don't engage in combat
Engage Mike in combat when encountered; react differently to different weapons; pose variable threat levels
Provides mission guidance across all time periods; identifies Tetrads; warns of threats
Appears only after Tetrads are assembled; provides final exposition; departs with Argonians to rebuild

notes: Zoda's Revenge employs a time-travel narrative structure where visual environmental design serves as primary world-state communication. Each time period is visually distinct, and the recurring presence of Zoda clones across eras creates narrative cohesion. The game uses character sprites of famous historical and fictional figures to ground time-period authenticity and mark safe interaction zones. Weapon acquisition is tied to specific historical figures, creating gameplay-narrative integration. The Tetrads (Tetris-piece-like objects, later renamed 'Blocks' for Virtual Console) function as both MacGuffins and world-state indicators—their gradual collection drives narrative progression. Enemy design reflects era appropriateness, reinforcing environmental storytelling. The return to C-Island in the final chapter provides visual narrative closure by revisiting the original game's setting.
